questions.question: A company needs to search internal policy documents using natural language questions. Which design pattern is most appropriate?

choices.explanation: RAG retrieves relevant context from a knowledge base and supplies it to a model to generate grounded answers.
Blue/green deployment is a release strategy and does not address document-grounded question answering.
Round-robin DNS distributes traffic and is not an AI retrieval pattern.
Encryption protects data at rest but does not enable natural language document search.

questions.question: Which AWS services or features can support a RAG application on AWS? Select all that apply.

choices.explanation: Knowledge Bases for Amazon Bedrock can connect data sources, retrieve relevant context, and support RAG workflows.
Vector search can store and retrieve embeddings for semantically similar content.
AWS WAF helps protect web applications but does not provide retrieval or generation capabilities by itself.

choices.text: Amazon S3 as a source for documents
choices.explanation: S3 can store source documents that are ingested into a knowledge base or retrieval pipeline.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of model evaluation before deploying an AI feature?

choices.text: To confirm quality, safety, and task performance against expected criteria.
choices.explanation: Evaluation checks whether a model meets functional and responsible AI expectations before users rely on it.

choices.text: To remove all need for user feedback.
choices.explanation: User feedback is still valuable after evaluation because real-world usage can expose new issues.

choices.text: To guarantee the model will never hallucinate.
choices.explanation: Evaluation reduces risk but cannot guarantee that generative models will never produce incorrect output.
Security testing remains necessary for any production application.
